acceptance_criteria.text: Agent channels have separate credentials from the renderer/session token, with channel identity, enabled state, creation time, rotation time, and revocation state.
The backend authenticates agent-channel message creation by channel credential and rejects missing, invalid, disabled, revoked, or mismatched credentials.
Rate limits exist per channel and urgency class, with deterministic failure responses and no sensitive body logging.
Settings UI lets the human view channel identities, create/rotate/revoke credentials, disable channels, and see recent rejection/rate-limit counts without exposing secrets.
Backup/export/retention behavior for channel metadata is explicit, tested, and documented, including which secret material is excluded or encrypted.
Tests cover valid channel auth, invalid auth, revoked channel, disabled channel, urgency abuse, rate-limit reset, UI redaction, and migration from the current launch-token-only model.
